column2: # BLOCK D — Complier covariate profiles vs. full sample
#   Estimate the covariate profile of the latent complier population.
#       D_i = treatment, e.g. somecol or educ16
#   This estimates "what compliers look like", not "who is a complier".
#   For binary X, the complier mean is the estimated share of compliers
#   kappa_i is signed. Therefore, finite-sample estimates for dummy variables
#   can leave [0,1]. This is not a literal percentage anymore; it is a sign
#   that the kappa reconstruction is unstable.
# Helper: Abadie kappa weights for complier covariate means
print_complier_profile <- function(D, D_name, p, p_name, covar_names) {
kappa_cov <- kappa_complier(Z = Z, D = D, p = p)
comp_mn  <- sum(kappa_cov * x_i, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(kappa_cov, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("  Full      = mean in the full sample.\n")
cat("  D=1       = mean among observed treated individuals.\n")
cat("  Complier  = kappa-weighted estimate of E[X | complier].\n")
cat("  If a dummy complier mean is outside [0,1], the kappa reconstruction is unstable.\n\n")
# Version 1: Unconditional propensity score p = P(Z = 1)
Treatment: D1 = somecol | Propensity score: marginal P(Z=1)
Full      = mean in the full sample.
D=1       = mean among observed treated individuals.
Complier  = kappa-weighted estimate of E[X | complier].
If a dummy complier mean is outside [0,1], the kappa reconstruction is unstable.
Treatment: D2 = educ16 | Propensity score: marginal P(Z=1)
Full      = mean in the full sample.
D=1       = mean among observed treated individuals.
Complier  = kappa-weighted estimate of E[X | complier].
If a dummy complier mean is outside [0,1], the kappa reconstruction is unstable.
Treatment: D1 = somecol | Propensity score: Card logit p(X)
Full      = mean in the full sample.
D=1       = mean among observed treated individuals.
Complier  = kappa-weighted estimate of E[X | complier].
If a dummy complier mean is outside [0,1], the kappa reconstruction is unstable.
Treatment: D2 = educ16 | Propensity score: Card logit p(X)
Full      = mean in the full sample.
D=1       = mean among observed treated individuals.
Complier  = kappa-weighted estimate of E[X | complier].
If a dummy complier mean is outside [0,1], the kappa reconstruction is unstable.
